marketFocus: Iran end-war pivot: Trump told NBC 'it is coming to an end' and suggested the US will leave Iran in 2-3 weeks. Iranian President Pezeshkian said Iran 'seeks no war but is prepared to end it with guarantees.' Cross-asset reaction: SPX +2.92%, NDX +3.43%, DXY weakened, oil sold off sharply, T-notes rallied. Trump to deliver nationwide address on Iran tonight at 02:00 FFT (Thu). Strikes continue overnight — US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, Baghdad, Erbil hit; tanker struck near Doha.

Eurozone CPI Flash MISSED: 2.5% YoY (vs 2.8% exp, prev 1.9%). Core CPI Flash: 2.3% YoY (vs 2.4% exp). Energy shock not yet filtering through into broader prices — ECB 'wait and see' approach reinforced. ECB Muller: 'hike probable in coming quarters.' ECB Rehn: 'hike not guaranteed.' EUR/USD holding 1.1500 but CPI miss caps near-term upside.

Japanese Tankan Large Manufacturing BEAT: 17 (vs 16 exp) — highest in 5+ years. Tankan Non-Manufacturing BEAT: 36 (vs 33 exp). BoJ April hike case strengthening. USD/JPY slid below 159.00. Key events today: USD ADP 14:15 FFT, USD Retail Sales 14:30 FFT, USD ISM Manufacturing 16:00 FFT. BoC Summary of Deliberations 19:30 FFT.

USD.tone: DXY Weakened on Iran End-War Hopes — ADP Missed, Risk-On Reduces Haven Demand
USD.summary: USD is Bearish today. DXY weakened as risk appetite surged on Iran end-war optimism — Trump told NBC the war 'is coming to an end' and suggested US will leave Iran in 2-3 weeks. Haven demand for USD evaporated as equities surged (SPX +2.92%) and oil sold off. ADP Non-Farm Employment Change missed badly: 41K (vs 63K exp) — a clear labour market softening signal. Retail Sales beat (0.5% vs 0.3% exp) and ISM Manufacturing came in near expectations (52.3 vs 52.4 exp) — partial offsets but insufficient to reverse the bearish USD tone. Trump's nationwide address tonight at 02:00 FFT is the key risk event. Fed's Schmid said 'inflation is the more salient risk' and there is a 'real risk inflation gets stuck near 3%' — hawkish but overwhelmed by geopolitical risk-on. Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bearish = NO alignment (mismatch).
drivers: DXY weakened on Iran end-war optimism — risk-on surge reduces haven demand for USD
ADP Non-Farm Employment Change MISSED: 41K (vs 63K exp) — labour market softening
Retail Sales BEAT: 0.5% MoM (vs 0.3% exp); Core Retail Sales in line at 0.3%
ISM Manufacturing PMI: 52.3 (vs 52.4 exp) — near expectations, still expansionary
Fed Schmid: 'inflation is the more salient risk; real risk inflation gets stuck near 3%'

EUR.tone: Eurozone CPI Flash MISSED 2.5% YoY — ECB Wait-and-See Reinforced, EUR/USD at 1.1500
EUR.summary: EUR is Neutral today. The Eurozone CPI Flash missed expectations: 2.5% YoY (vs 2.8% exp, prev 1.9%). Core CPI Flash also missed: 2.3% YoY (vs 2.4% exp). The data indicates the Iran energy shock has not yet filtered through into broader price pressures — the ECB's 'wait and see' approach is reinforced. EUR/USD is holding the 1.1500 handle supported by a weaker dollar, but the CPI miss caps near-term upside for EUR. ECB Muller said a hike is 'probable in coming quarters' and ECB Rehn said it is 'not guaranteed.' ECB Radev warned of 'durable imprint' from past inflation shocks. German institutes cut 2026 growth forecasts to 0.6% (from 1.2%) citing the Iran war. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: Eurozone CPI Flash MISSED: 2.5% YoY (vs 2.8% exp) — energy shock not yet filtering through
Eurozone Core CPI Flash MISSED: 2.3% YoY (vs 2.4% exp) — ECB wait-and-see reinforced
ECB Muller: 'hike probable in coming quarters'; ECB Rehn: 'hike not guaranteed'
EUR/USD holding 1.1500 on weaker dollar — CPI miss caps near-term EUR upside
German institutes cut 2026 growth to 0.6% (prev 1.2%) — Iran war and energy costs cited

GBP.tone: UK GDP Confirmed + Current Account Beat + National Living Wage Rise — GBP Resilient
GBP.summary: GBP is Bullish today. UK Final GDP Q4 confirmed at 0.1% QoQ (no downward revision) and 1.0% YoY — in line with expectations, providing stability. UK Current Account beat significantly: -18.4B (vs -23.3B exp) — a positive structural signal for GBP. The UK national living wage rose to £12.71 today and energy bills are cut by an average of £117/year — cost-of-living relief measures support domestic consumption. GBP/USD is rebounding from the 1.3200 level. BOE repricing (~81bps tightening priced) remains intact. The DXY weakness on Iran end-war optimism is providing additional tailwind for GBP. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment, but GBP is the cleanest bullish story in the G10 today.
drivers: UK Final GDP Q4 confirmed at 0.1% QoQ — no downward revision; stability signal
UK Current Account BEAT: -18.4B (vs -23.3B exp) — positive structural GBP signal
UK national living wage rises to £12.71 today; energy bills cut £117/yr — cost-of-living relief
GBP/USD rebounding from 1.3200 — DXY weakness on Iran end-war optimism provides tailwind
BOE repricing (~81bps tightening priced) intact; no fresh catalyst needed to hold bullish bias

JPY.tone: Tankan Large Manufacturing BEAT 17 — BoJ April Hike Case Strengthening, USD/JPY Below 159
JPY.summary: JPY is Bullish today. The BoJ Tankan Large Manufacturing Index beat expectations: 17 (vs 16 exp) — the highest reading in more than five years. The Tankan Non-Manufacturing Index also beat: 36 (vs 33 exp). This data strongly supports the case for a BoJ rate hike at the April meeting. USD/JPY slid below the 159.00 level as the dollar weakened on Iran end-war optimism and the Tankan data reinforced BoJ hawkishness. Japan spent JPY 0 on FX interventions between February 26 and March 27 — confirming no intervention pressure. ING: 'Tokyo CPI miss will not deter BoJ April hike; surging oil, weak JPY, and rising Shunto wages all favour near-term hike.' BoJ also reduced its planned JGB purchases for Q2 — a further tightening signal. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment, but JPY is fundamentally supported.
drivers: Tankan Large Manufacturing BEAT: 17 (vs 16 exp) — highest in 5+ years; BoJ April hike supported
Tankan Non-Manufacturing BEAT: 36 (vs 33 exp) — services sector strength intact
USD/JPY slid below 159.00 — dollar weakness on Iran end-war optimism amplifies JPY gains
BoJ reduced Q2 JGB purchase plan — further tightening signal; JPY 0 spent on interventions
ING: 'surging oil, weak JPY, and rising Shunto wages all favour near-term BoJ hike'

AUD.tone: ASX 200 Rallied but China RatingDog PMI Missed — AUD Mixed Between Risk-On and Weak Data
AUD.summary: AUD is Neutral today. The ASX 200 rallied strongly on Iran end-war optimism, led by mining, materials, and tech — a positive signal for AUD via the equity channel. However, the Chinese RatingDog Manufacturing PMI missed: 50.8 (vs 51.6 exp, prev 52.1) — a disappointment for AUD's key trade partner. Australian Manufacturing PMI Final came in at 49.8 (below 50 = contraction). Antipodeans were mixed and rangebound, unable to fully benefit from the risk-on environment. The RBA Minutes from Tuesday showed a divided board — 'oil at $100 would lift CPI to ~5% in June quarter.' AUD/USD is rangebound. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: ASX 200 rallied on Iran end-war optimism — mining, materials, tech led gains
Chinese RatingDog Manufacturing PMI MISSED: 50.8 (vs 51.6 exp) — key trade partner miss
Australian Manufacturing PMI Final: 49.8 (below 50 = contraction) — domestic weakness
Antipodeans rangebound; AUD/USD unable to fully benefit from risk-on environment
RBA Minutes: 'oil at $100 would lift CPI to ~5% in June quarter' — stagflation risk intact

CAD.tone: Oil Sold Off Sharply on Iran End-War Hopes — CAD Loses Primary Driver, BoC Minutes Tonight
CAD.summary: CAD is Bearish today. Oil sold off sharply as Iran end-war optimism dominated: WTI fell from the $106 range toward $100, Brent retreated from $112. CAD is a pure petro-proxy and loses its primary bullish driver when oil sells off. The directional momentum for oil is now bearish — if Trump's nationwide address tonight confirms a ceasefire framework, oil could fall further and drag CAD lower. BoC Summary of Deliberations at 19:30 FFT is the key domestic event — any dovish signals would compound CAD weakness. US Private Inventory Data showed a crude build of +10.3mln bbls (vs -1.3mln exp) — a bearish oil signal. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment, but CAD is the clearest bearish story today.
drivers: Oil sold off sharply on Iran end-war optimism — WTI retreated from $106 toward $100
US Private Inventory Data: Crude +10.3mln bbls (vs -1.3mln exp) — bearish oil signal
BoC Summary of Deliberations at 19:30 FFT — dovish signals would compound CAD weakness
CAD is a pure petro-proxy — loses primary bullish driver when oil directional momentum turns
Trump nationwide address tonight at 02:00 FFT — ceasefire framework would push oil lower

CHF.tone: Risk-On Reduces Haven Demand — SNB Cap Intact, ECB CPI Miss Reduces EUR/CHF Pressure
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. Risk-on sentiment from Iran end-war optimism reduces haven demand for CHF. USD/CHF is stabilizing as the dollar weakens but CHF does not benefit from the risk-on environment. The Eurozone CPI Flash miss (2.5% vs 2.8% exp) reduces the pressure for EUR/CHF to move higher — EUR/CHF is rangebound near 0.9100. SNB intervention cap remains intact. ECB Radev warned of 'durable imprint' from past inflation shocks — a hawkish signal that limits CHF appreciation vs EUR. No Swiss data today. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅ — but no bullish aligned counterpart to pair against CHF.
drivers: Risk-on from Iran end-war optimism reduces haven demand for CHF
SNB intervention cap intact — limits CHF appreciation against EUR and USD
Eurozone CPI Flash miss reduces EUR/CHF upside pressure — pair rangebound near 0.9100
ECB Radev: 'past inflation shocks leave durable imprint' — hawkish signal limits CHF vs EUR
No Swiss data today — CHF trading purely on global risk sentiment dynamics

NZD.tone: Antipodeans Mixed — China PMI Miss Weighs, Risk-On Supports, No Domestic Catalyst
NZD.summary: NZD is Neutral today. Antipodeans were mixed and rangebound. The risk-on environment from Iran end-war optimism provides some support for NZD as a high-beta currency. However, the Chinese RatingDog Manufacturing PMI missed (50.8 vs 51.6 exp) — a negative for NZD via the China trade channel. NZD/USD is rangebound. No domestic NZD catalyst today. RBNZ stagflation warning remains intact. The competing forces of risk-on (positive) and weak China data (negative) leave NZD in a neutral holding pattern. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: Risk-on from Iran end-war optimism provides support for NZD as high-beta currency
Chinese RatingDog Manufacturing PMI MISSED: 50.8 (vs 51.6 exp) — negative for NZD
Antipodeans rangebound; NZD/USD unable to find clear direction
RBNZ stagflation warning intact — energy shock raises inflation while weighing on growth
No domestic NZD catalyst today — pure risk sentiment and China data proxy

noSetupsReason: No High Conviction Setups today. Only CHF has Weekly-Daily alignment (both Bearish), but there are no Bullish aligned currencies to pair against it. USD Weekly Bullish conflicts with Daily Bearish. EUR Weekly Bullish but Daily Neutral. GBP and JPY are Daily Bullish but Weekly Neutral. No directional alignment exists for a valid swing setup.
